summary: Request to make SKGLView.Dispose() virtual in the Blazor implementation so that subclasses can properly override disposal behavior.

stepsToReproduce: Create a class that inherits from SKGLView in a Blazor WebAssembly project
Attempt to override or extend the Dispose method
Observe that Dispose is non-virtual and cannot be overridden

repoLinks.description: Reporter-linked source showing non-virtual Dispose method

analysis.summary: SKGLView.Dispose() at line 190 is a plain public method implementing IDisposable directly, without the standard Dispose(bool) pattern. The class is not sealed, so subclassing is allowed, but Dispose cannot be overridden. SKCanvasView.razor.cs has the identical non-virtual pattern at line 171. Implementing the standard IDisposable Dispose(bool disposing) pattern would fix both classes.
analysis.rationale: This is an enhancement request to improve an existing API surface. The class is explicitly designed for inheritance (not sealed, Blazor component) but the Dispose method doesn't follow the standard .NET disposable pattern. The reporter has a concrete use case (subclassing SKGLView) blocked by this limitation. The same issue applies to SKCanvasView.

codeInvestigation.finding: Class is declared as 'public partial class SKGLView : IDisposable' — not sealed, so subclassing is permitted
Dispose() is 'public void Dispose()' — non-virtual, no Dispose(bool) pattern, disposes dpiWatcher, sizeWatcher, and interop without null-checks
SKCanvasView has the same non-virtual Dispose pattern but uses null-conditional operators (?.) and also calls FreeBitmap() — same enhancement would apply here

keySignals.text: SKGLView in blazor implementation has a non-virtual Dispose method, which creates problems when subclassing it
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter has a concrete subclassing scenario blocked by the current design

resolution.hypothesis: Both Blazor view classes implement IDisposable without the standard Dispose(bool) pattern, preventing proper disposal extension in subclasses.

proposals.title: Implement standard Dispose(bool) pattern
proposals.description: Refactor SKGLView (and SKCanvasView) to use the standard .NET Dispose pattern: make Dispose() call a protected virtual Dispose(bool disposing) method, allowing subclasses to override and extend disposal.

proposals.title: Use 'new' keyword to shadow Dispose
proposals.description: As a workaround, subclass authors can shadow the Dispose method using the 'new' keyword, though this breaks polymorphic disposal through IDisposable references.

resolution.recommendedReason: Follows .NET best practices (CA1063), is ABI-safe (adds new protected virtual method), and fixes the same gap in both SKGLView and SKCanvasView.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with clear implementation path. Needs verification that the Dispose(bool) pattern integrates correctly with Blazor component lifecycle.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and Blazor views labels
Acknowledge the request and confirm the gap applies to both Blazor views
Link to related Blazor Dispose issue #3322
